packet.name: L (Stanford A, Imperial B, Rutgers A, Dartmouth A, Duke A, Penn A)

tossups.question: In 2021, this architect installed 49 dead cedar trees in Madison Square Park in Ghost Forest, an installation that is part of this architect’s What Is Missing? project. A sculpture of nurses by Glenna Goodacre was built as a “Women’s” counterpart to a design by this architect. This architect was inspired by the Serpent Mounds to create “earth drawings” such as The Wave Field. This architect imagined “taking a knife and cutting into the earth” for a project that beat Frederick Hart’s design in a 1981 competition. While an undergraduate at Yale, this architect designed two black granite walls where visitors rub the inscribed names of deceased soldiers onto paper. For 10 points, name this architect who designed Washington DC’s Vietnam Veterans Memorial.
tossups.question_sanitized: In 2021, this architect installed 49 dead cedar trees in Madison Square Park in Ghost Forest, an installation that is part of this architect's What Is Missing? project. A sculpture of nurses by Glenna Goodacre was built as a "Women's" counterpart to a design by this architect. This architect was inspired by the Serpent Mounds to create "earth drawings" such as The Wave Field. This architect imagined "taking a knife and cutting into the earth" for a project that beat Frederick Hart's design in a 1981 competition. While an undergraduate at Yale, this architect designed two black granite walls where visitors rub the inscribed names of deceased soldiers onto paper. For 10 points, name this architect who designed Washington DC's Vietnam Veterans Memorial.

tossups.question: A megaphone-shaped sculpture made of bronze and redwood by this artist is part of a series that includes an exhibit in which visitors use plexiglass to capture videos projected on the ceiling, the Empty Room. For the 2015 reopening of the Renwick, this artist glued 54,000 glass marbles in the shape of the Anacostia Watershed to create Folding of the Chesapeake. Listening Cone is part of this artist’s “last memorial,” an ongoing project to document endangered and extinct species entitled What Is Missing?. This artist’s “Earth Drawings” include the Serpent Mound-like Eleven Minute Line. The UMich campus, Miami, and the Storm King Art Center are the sites of three square plots of (*) undulating land by this artist, all titled Wavefield. The conical table in this artist’s Civil Rights Memorial uses the same material as a monument to which the statue Three Soldiers was added. For 10 points, name this artist who as an undergraduate used black marble in her design of the Vietnam Memorial.
tossups.question_sanitized: A megaphone-shaped sculpture made of bronze and redwood by this artist is part of a series that includes an exhibit in which visitors use plexiglass to capture videos projected on the ceiling, the Empty Room. For the 2015 reopening of the Renwick, this artist glued 54,000 glass marbles in the shape of the Anacostia Watershed to create Folding of the Chesapeake. Listening Cone is part of this artist's "last memorial," an ongoing project to document endangered and extinct species entitled What Is Missing?. This artist's "Earth Drawings" include the Serpent Mound-like Eleven Minute Line. The UMich campus, Miami, and the Storm King Art Center are the sites of three square plots of (*) undulating land by this artist, all titled Wavefield. The conical table in this artist's Civil Rights Memorial uses the same material as a monument to which the statue Three Soldiers was added. For 10 points, name this artist who as an undergraduate used black marble in her design of the Vietnam Memorial.

tossups.question: This artist used beeswax to depict the phases of the moon in one portable sculpture, and gathered ground-up recycled safety glass into mounds for Groundswell at the Wexner Center for the Arts. This designer of the reading garden at the Cleveland Public Library created a black stone fountain inspired by a Martin Luther King quote in Montgomery, Alabama and an eleven-acre field of undulating grass hills at the University of Michigan. This creator of Wave Field and the Civil Rights Memorial designed another work that was supplemented by Frederick Hart’s Three Soldiers sculpture and consists of black granite walls inscribed with over 58,000 names. For 10 points, identify this designer of the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist used beeswax to depict the phases of the moon in one portable sculpture, and gathered ground-up recycled safety glass into mounds for Groundswell at the Wexner Center for the Arts. This designer of the reading garden at the Cleveland Public Library created a black stone fountain inspired by a Martin Luther King quote in Montgomery, Alabama and an eleven-acre field of undulating grass hills at the University of Michigan. This creator of Wave Field and the Civil Rights Memorial designed another work that was supplemented by Frederick Hart's Three Soldiers sculpture and consists of black granite walls inscribed with over 58,000 names. For 10 points, identify this designer of the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.

bonuses.leadin: A series based around one of these locations called for installations inspired by Native American stories and notes from the Lewis and Clark Expedition. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these locations, one of which is the subject of the Confluence Project. An artificial park called Little Island was built in one of these natural features surrounding Manhattan.

The Confluence Project, which explores the history and culture of the Columbia River, was spearheaded by this architect. This architect inscribed the names of female students at Yale onto a slab of black granite.

Maya Lin used recycled silver for her 84-foot "Silver River" sculpture in this city. A building in this city took inspiration from The Wizard of Oz with an emerald green-striped facade and a yellow brick road lobby.
answers_sanitized: rivers [or the Columbia River; or the Hudson River]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A series based around one of these locations called for installations inspired by Native American stories and notes from the Lewis and Clark Expedition. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name these locations, one of which is the subject of the Confluence Project. An artificial park called Little Island was built in one of these natural features surrounding Manhattan.

The Confluence Project, which explores the history and culture of the Columbia River, was spearheaded by this architect. This architect inscribed the names of female students at Yale onto a slab of black granite.

Maya Lin used recycled silver for her 84-foot "Silver River" sculpture in this city. A building in this city took inspiration from The Wizard of Oz with an emerald green-striped facade and a yellow brick road lobby.

bonuses.leadin: Martin Hill criss-crossed sticks of raupo fiber for his piece Synergy, which is located in one of these places. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify these places. For a different site-specific work in one of these places, Robert Smithson arranged over 6,000 tons of black basalt in the shape of a spiral.

Robert Smithson also wrote an essay documenting how installing these objects affected his surroundings in the Yucatán. Phalli’s Field was the first in a series of art installations of rooms covered with these objects.
Inspired by the Adena and Hopewell mounds of her home state, this artist dabbled in land art with her Wavefields series. This sculptor designed the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.
answers: lakes [accept Lake Wanaka or Great Salt Lake; prompt on bodies of water]
mirrors (The essay is “Incidents of Mirror-Travel in the Yucatan.” The second series is Yayoi Kusama’s Infinity Mirror Rooms.)
answers_sanitized: lakes [accept Lake Wanaka or Great Salt Lake; prompt on bodies of water]
mirrors (The essay is "Incidents of Mirror-Travel in the Yucatan." The second series is Yayoi Kusama's Infinity Mirror Rooms.)

parts_sanitized: Identify these places. For a different site-specific work in one of these places, Robert Smithson arranged over 6,000 tons of black basalt in the shape of a spiral.

Robert Smithson also wrote an essay documenting how installing these objects affected his surroundings in the Yucatan. Phalli's Field was the first in a series of art installations of rooms covered with these objects.
Inspired by the Adena and Hopewell mounds of her home state, this artist dabbled in land art with her Wavefields series. This sculptor designed the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about architect Deborah Berke, for 10 points each:
parts: Berke is the first female dean of this university’s architecture school, housed in the early Brutalist Rudolph Hall. Richard Rogers and Norman Foster met at this Ivy League university, whose “whale-like” hockey rink was designed by Eero Saarinen.

As dean, Berke has praised this Yale alumnus’s works, such as The Woman’s Table, which contains a spiral to denote Yale’s rising female enrollment. This architect designed the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.

In 2017, Berke’s firm restored one of these buildings in Buffalo into the wildly popular Hotel Henry. In the 1800s, these buildings were designed with a “bat-wing” like floor plan and lots of natural light and ventilation based on the Kirkbride principles.
answers: mental asylums [or insane asylums; or psychiatric hospitals; or mental hospitals; prompt on hospitals]
answers_sanitized: mental asylums [or insane asylums; or psychiatric hospitals; or mental hospitals; prompt on hospitals]

As dean, Berke has praised this Yale alumnus's works, such as The Woman's Table, which contains a spiral to denote Yale's rising female enrollment. This architect designed the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.
